title: winy shaggy middle-eastern seafood en-croute
description: a hearty, comforting dish that showcases the flavors of the middle east. this winey, shaggy seafood en-croute is a nostalgic treat that's perfect for a cozy night in.
instructions: in a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the chopped onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
add the seafood stock to the skillet and bring to a boil.
reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
in a separate bowl, mix together the sautéed onion and seafood stock mixture.
roll out 1 package of puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
spread the onion and seafood stock mixture evenly over the center of the pastry, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
brush the edges of the pastry with a little water, then fold the pastry over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
cut a few slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape.
place the en-croute on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
